f: Prashanth Neel's Kannada gold-mine epic was the most expensive Kannada film ever at the time and launched Yash as a pan-India star.
c: "Bombay mercenary promises his dying mother he'll die rich. Accepts a job killing a gold-mine boss in a Kolar pit. Stays for the job."

f: The sequel hit over 1,200 crore worldwide and made Sanjay Dutt's Adheera the year's most cosplayed Telugu-dub villain.
c: "Gold-mine heir keeps the throne by killing an uncle with a Viking beard. A woman prime minister in Delhi takes notes."

f: Rishab Shetty's folk-horror thriller was shot in coastal Karnataka with a real Bhoota Kola performance finale.
c: "Coastal Karnataka buffalo-race champion inherits a land dispute with a forest officer. A village deity settles it at the climax."

f: Rajinikanth's comeback vehicle featured Mohanlal, Shiva Rajkumar, and Jackie Shroff in cameos across three film industries.
c: "Retired prison warden grandpa hunts down an idol-smuggler gang after they grab his cop son. Hums a song. Keeps hunting."

f: Second film in the Lokesh Cinematic Universe. Kamal Haasan reprised a 1986 role and Suriya's cameo ended the film.
c: "Retired black-ops agent fakes his own death. Drug syndicate learns that was a bad assumption. Son's cop squad keeps dying meanwhile."

f: Trivikram's Sankranti 2024 release grossed 170 crore but got panned for its thin mother-son plot and divisive second half.
c: "Andhra chilli trader with mom issues stonewalls a grandfather's political empire. Resolves a thirty-year family estrangement at a wedding."

f: V. V. Vinayak's debut and NTR Jr.'s first solo blockbuster. It locked in the Rayalaseema faction template for a decade.
c: "Young man with a haircut comes back to a Rayalaseema village to kill the man who killed his father. Does it over a long interval."

f: India's first major sci-fi film with a time machine. Singeetam Srinivasa Rao paired Balakrishna with Ilayaraja and dialogues by Jandhyala.
c: "Scientist builds a time machine shaped like a car. It takes a college gang to Krishnadevaraya's court and a post-nuclear wasteland."

f: Naveen Polishetty co-wrote and starred in this Nellore-set indie that made detective agencies funny again and won him the Nandi Award.
c: "Nellore detective with three client reviews finds a dead body near railway tracks. Decides one low-paying case is worth dying for."

f: Nandini Reddy's debut and Nani's first lead. Told its love story backward from a wedding, a format rare in Telugu cinema at the time.
c: "Two strangers meet at a wedding. They remember a decade of near-misses. Narrated in reverse for no particular reason."

f: Kodandarami Reddy's comedy was remade in Tamil as Galatta Maappillai and in Kannada as Gadibidi Aliya.
c: "Young groom moves into in-laws' mansion. Charms three warring daughters. Each falls for him in rotation."

f: Sekhar Kammula used his Howard University MFA thesis script to make a film that reset Telugu romance for a decade.
c: "Divorcee woman meets a patient tenant. They chat. They drink filter coffee. She slowly stops flinching."

f: Raghavendra Rao's hagiography swept eight Nandi Awards, three Filmfare South, and won Nagarjuna the National Film Award.
c: "A 15th-century Tirupati composer writes 32,000 songs. Sings most of them at the deity. Sings a few at earthly kings too."

f: Muthyala Subbaiah's hit was a Telugu adaptation of the Rajkumar Kannada original and gave Chiranjeevi a rare family-drama lead.
c: "Eldest brother raises three siblings after the parents die. Sacrifices everything. Cries at exactly one wedding per reel."

f: Trivikram's faction drama was Thaman's 100th score and NTR Jr.'s first film after Janatha Garage to cross 150 crore.
c: "Rayalaseema faction heir returns from abroad for his father's funeral. Decides the only fix for generational killing is therapy."

f: Kodi Ramakrishna's horror-fantasy built a full period palace set in Hyderabad and launched Anushka Shetty as a commercial lead.
c: "Bride discovers she is the reincarnation of her warrior great-grandmother. An evil uncle-villain wakes up too. Palace fight."

f: Sukumar's directorial debut ran 100 days in 150 centres and made Allu Arjun's dyed-blonde hair the decade's most copied college look.
c: "Unhinged college romantic keeps courting a girl who's already engaged. Announces his love at assembly. The friend quietly loses."

f: Sukumar's self-described spiritual sequel is studied in Telugu film circles as a pre-Arjun Reddy portrait of obsessive love.
c: "Orphan grows up poisoning his adopted brother. Falls for brother's girlfriend. Keeps framing it as affection."

f: Puri Jagannadh's directorial debut was Amisha Patel's South debut and gave Pawan Kalyan his first mass-hero template.
c: "College boy with attitude follows a rich girl across a town. Threatens her fiance. Claims it is love."

f: G. Ashok's bilingual horror was shot simultaneously in Telugu and Tamil. Anushka played a district collector accused of murder.
c: "District-collector prisoner is interrogated in a haunted bungalow. The mansion keeps interrupting. Cops pretend not to notice."

f: Anil Ravipudi wrote the role for Balakrishna after NBK 107. Sreeleela's role was positioned as an adopted daughter subplot.
c: "Prison convict becomes foster father to a girl. Trains her to join the army. Fights a politician at every breakfast."

f: Singeetam Srinivasa Rao's high-fantasy classic shot song sequences on imaginary flying rocks and popularised green-screen in Telugu cinema.
c: "Tribal-raised prince rescues a princess from a sorcerer living on a floating island. Blows a conch. Fight on clouds."

f: Maruthi's sleeper hit made Nani's absent-minded-plant-scientist a meme and was remade in Hindi as Jabariya Jodi.
c: "Botanist with severe short-term memory loss hides it from his Kuchipudi-dancer fiancée. Forgets her name mid-engagement."

f: B. Gopal's blockbuster launched Divya Bharti in Telugu cinema at age 16 and ran 175 days in multiple centres.
c: "Village-faction scion elopes with a cousin's fiancée. A long feud restarts. Everyone carries a sword to every family function."

f: Puri Jagannadh's Ram Charan launch was an unofficial remake of Guy Ritchie's Swept Away and hit 25 crore globally.
c: "Rich kid washes up on an island with the don who killed his father. Trains for revenge between coconut scenes."

f: NTR directed, produced, and played three roles: Karna, Duryodhana and Krishna. The film ran over four hours at release.
c: "Mahabharata re-told from the charioteer son's view. One man plays the hero, the friend, and the god talking to the hero."

f: A. Karunakaran's rom-com hit 40 crore and made the Prabhas-Kajal pair a fan favourite. The Swiss Alps shoot cost a third of the budget.
c: "Boy invents a long-lost girlfriend to escape an arranged marriage to a gangster's daughter. Goes to Switzerland. The lie grows legs."

f: Bharat Kamma's debut. Its student-union rage and breakup arc turned Rashmika Mandanna's state-cricketer role into a career pivot.
c: "Angry student-union leader loses his girlfriend after her concert-hall assault. Takes three years to learn the word therapy."

f: Boyapati Srinu's hit reset Balakrishna's post-50 career and became 2021's second-biggest Telugu release after Pushpa.
c: "Twin brothers: one a village protector, one a jungle-dwelling Aghori. The village one loses a fight. The jungle one wakes up."

f: V. Vijayendra Prasad drew loosely on freedom fighter Suddala Hanmanthu and the Razakar movement. The film won six Nandi Awards.
c: "Orphan girl walks to Nehru's Delhi to sing her dead father's freedom songs for the nation. A Hyderabad Nizam landlord tries to stop her."

f: Jandhyala's Rajendra Prasad comedy was adapted from Adivishnu's novel and took its title from a Mayabazar song.
c: "Miser's grandson wants to marry the girl next door. Grandfather refuses to part with a single rupee. Entire village plots around him."

f: The Ramana-Bapu adaptation of Uttara Ramayana won two National Film Awards and remains a Doordarshan-era staple.
c: "Queen is exiled for a rumour about her chastity. Raises twin sons in a sage's ashram. They grow up and meet the king at a horse sacrifice."

f: Sujeeth's first pan-India film was shot across UAE, Romania, and Serbia on a 350-crore budget, a Telugu industry record.
c: "Two undercover cops chase a 2,000-crore heist. Find an underworld war. The plot changes city three times. The cars keep flipping."

f: Puri Jagannadh's Mahesh Babu film grossed over 70 crore and was based on an original story by Ram Gopal Varma.
c: "Young man arrives in Mumbai on a train. Decides to replace the top don within a month. Wears the same blazer throughout."

f: Prabhu Deva's directorial debut. Set in the 1960s, it centred on a temple dance ritual performed once every full moon.
c: "Mysterious dancer trains a young woman for a temple ritual performed on the full moon. A long buried family secret emerges."

f: Bhaskar's romantic drama flopped hard on release but became a cult classic for arguing that love has an expiry date.
c: "Street artist tells his girlfriend love expires like fruit. She accepts the theory. He spends two hours regretting the theory."

f: Vivek Athreya's vigilante concept came from Malladi's novel about a man who beats up bullies only on Saturdays.
c: "Man only beats up abusers on Saturdays. A sadistic cop finds out. Their shared calendar becomes the problem."

f: EVV Satyanarayana's remake of Twin Dragons was 1994's top Telugu grosser. It was remade in Hindi as Judwaa and Judwaa 2.
c: "Twins separated at birth. One is a rowdy, one is a musician. They swap cities and swap girlfriends without meeting."

f: Raghavendra Rao paired Chiranjeevi with Sobhana and Divya Bharti. The dual role cemented Chiranjeevi's 90s star template.
c: "Goonda son-in-law is actually twins. One wears a headband, one wears a suit. Both marry into the same family."

f: B. Gopal's Balakrishna actioner was 1992's biggest Telugu grosser and ran 100 days in 30 centres.
c: "Rule-breaking thanedar slaps corrupt politicians before reading their statements. Transferred seven times. Keeps slapping."

n: Naa Peru Surya, Naa Illu India
f: Vakkantham Vamsi's directorial debut. Army-veteran father issues a courtroom-style challenge to his anger-issue soldier son.
c: "Soldier with anger issues is ordered by a court-martial to fix his temper to rejoin the border unit. Yells through every single fix."

f: Puri Jagannadh shot the entire film in Spain and Paris and dubbed it in Malayalam as Romeo & Juliets.
c: "Guitarist in Barcelona falls for one girl. Then another. Flashback reveals both are tied to the same Spanish villain."

f: Santosh Srinivas's debut ran 100 days and was remade in Hindi as Main Tera Hero, starring Varun Dhawan.
c: "Hyderabad boy in Vizag university accidentally saves a minister's daughter from a fake kidnap. Her brothers arrive with more blades than words."

f: Surender Reddy's sequel paired Ravi Teja with himself in a father-son dual role using split-screen VFX throughout.
c: "Adrenaline-junkie father returns from the dead in his son. Son inherits taste for fights and factory-strength antagonists."

f: Gopichand Malineni based the screenplay on real incidents from Andhra-Telangana crime files and framed it around a Tagaru-style narration.
c: "Circle inspector collects three open cases across decades. Each solves the same gangster. Keeps a wall of photos."

f: Sampath Nandi's debut with Ram Charan was 2012's highest-grossing Telugu film and marked two Tamil actors' Telugu debuts.
c: "Small-time orphan runs a goons-for-rent racket in Kolkata. Turns out to be the missing heir of a Hyderabad godfather."

f: A. Karunakaran's college rom-com became a cult film on the strength of Yuvan Shankar Raja's score and the Allu Arjun-Genelia chemistry.
c: "Cheerful college senior falls for a girl already engaged to a Mumbai don. Sings four monsoon songs to convince her."

f: V. V. Vinayak's film was Allu Arjun's first solo mass lead and sold 40+ crore on opening weekend.
c: "College boy loves and loses the same girl at three different stages. Each breakup ends with a group-dance routine."

f: Trinadha Rao's film was Ravi Teja's comeback hit after three flops and ran 50 days in Andhra-Telangana despite mixed reviews.
c: "Unemployed man is mistaken for a billionaire heir. Doesn't correct anyone. Marries the heir's fiancée by accident."

f: Sreenu Vaitla's NTR Jr. actioner was Thaman's second Sreenu Vaitla soundtrack and grossed over 55 crore.
c: "Dubai-based contract killer is hired to take down a Hyderabad don. Falls for the don's sister. Continues the contract anyway."

f: Parasuram's Mahesh Babu film opened with a 60-crore weekend and framed the plot around a USA-based microfinance lender.
c: "US microfinance agent chases an MP's daughter across two continents for her unpaid gambling debt. Collects interest in action set-pieces."

f: Anil Ravipudi's Dussehra-2017 release grossed 60 crore and featured Ravi Teja playing a blind man for the entire film.
c: "Blind son promises his dead mother he'll protect a girl. Finds her in a hill town. Beats up a gang that can see just fine."

f: Puri Jagannadh produced and directed this Nithiin romance and shot the entire thing in Barcelona and Germany.
c: "Commitment-phobic dancer in Barcelona dates three women simultaneously. His uncle is a doctor specialising in cardiac events."

f: Surender Reddy's NTR Jr. actioner was Vidyut Jammwal's Telugu debut and used the chameleon as the lead's shapeshifting motif.
c: "Con artist changes identities like a chameleon. Falls for a billionaire's daughter. Keeps forgetting which name he gave her."

f: Boyapati Srinu's faction-drama crossed 100 crore and was dubbed in Hindi as Unstoppable Khiladi.
c: "Software engineer moonlights as a Rajasthan princess's bodyguard. Single-handedly kills a desert-kingdom family. Returns to a cubicle."

f: K. S. Ravikumar's Sankranti-2018 release was Balakrishna's 102nd film. It featured him adopting an orphaned child.
c: "Widower on the run hides in a village with his best friend's baby. The baby's family arrives. Fights follow every meal."

f: Rahul Sankrityan's debut horror-comedy was Vijay Deverakonda's first post-Arjun Reddy release and crossed 30 crore.
c: "Unemployed Hyderabad man buys a 1980s vintage taxi. Taxi is haunted by its previous owner. Taxi drives itself to murders."

f: Venkat Prabhu shot this bilingual period thriller in Telugu and Tamil. It flopped despite a dual Ilaiyaraaja-Yuvan score.
c: "1990s Tamil Nadu constable is ordered to transport a witness across the state. Everyone above his rank wants the witness dead."

f: Srikanth Addala's Telugu remake of Asuran was based on Poomani's novel Vekkai and shot entirely in Anantapur.
c: "Village farmer watches his son kill an upper-caste landlord. Runs into the forest with the boy. Teaches him when to hide and when to swing."

f: Chandoo Mondeti based the film on a real 2018 incident where Srikakulam fishermen were captured by Pakistan's maritime forces.
c: "Srikakulam fisherman drifts into Pakistani waters. Spends two years in a Karachi jail. Girlfriend waits by the beach."

f: Anil Ravipudi's standalone sequel to F2 grossed over 100 crore and marked the first post-pandemic Venkatesh-Varun Tej collab.
c: "Two greedy men find out a bank account of 150 crore is theirs. Every other character shows up to claim it."

f: Sailesh Kolanu's second HIT entry investigated dismembered bodies in Andhra Pradesh and confirmed the HIT films as a shared universe.
c: "Homicide cop eats biryani while inspecting a corpse. Investigation keeps producing more body parts. He keeps eating."

f: Sailesh Kolanu's third HIT installment crossed 50 crore in its opening weekend and introduced a serial-murder team set in the Himalayas.
c: "Ruthless homicide officer chases a group of killers operating across hill-station lodges. Writes notes on every lodge register."

f: Merlapaka Gandhi's hyperlink comedy was released on Sankranti 2016 and ran its multiple storylines on train-station tickets.
c: "Four strangers' lives collide over a lost suitcase at a railway station. Each thinks the suitcase is his."

f: Raj & DK produced Praveen Kandregula's debut about villagers shooting their own film after finding a camera. Premiered on Netflix.
c: "Village auto driver finds a stranger's camera. Decides to make a blockbuster with it. Hires the local tea-stall boy as hero."

f: Sujeeth's debut hit 40 crore and was later dubbed in Tamil with the same title.
c: "Petty conman befriends a blind girl. Her cop father happens to be chasing him. He keeps running in both directions."

f: Sekhar Kammula's coming-of-age drama used five debutants and featured future stars Vijay Deverakonda and Naveen Polisetty in bit roles.
c: "A middle-class Hyderabad colony's kids grow up together. A new family moves in. A decade passes in songs and street cricket."

f: The Telugu-dubbed release of Sudha Kongara's Soorarai Pottru. Inspired by low-cost-carrier founder G. R. Gopinath's memoir.
c: "Farmer's son from Madurai decides to start a budget airline so villagers can fly. Every tycoon and minister says no for ten years."

f: Venky Kudumula's Sankranti-2020 hit was produced for 20 crore and grossed over 60. Its organic-farm subplot became a TikTok trend.
c: "Social-media influencer with a weak heart is forced to manage an organic-farming brand. The boss's daughter keeps showing up."

f: Krishna Chaitanya's debut was produced by Pawan Kalyan and Trivikram Srinivas. Story credit went to Trivikram.
c: "Debt-dodging call-centre worker flies to the US to find his old flame. Finds her married. Tries to restart anyway."

f: K. S. Ravikumar directed this Balakrishna dual-role actioner that opened on Dussehra 2019 and grossed over 35 crore.
c: "Lookalike lawyer replaces a dead union minister. Runs the country from a Delhi bungalow. Nobody in Parliament notices."

f: Puri Jagannadh's film was Kangana Ranaut's South debut. Prabhas played a contract killer with a no-women-no-kids rule.
c: "Contract killer refuses jobs involving women. A girl hires him to kill her. He stalls. He stalls a lot."

f: Ashwath Marimuthu's Telugu remake of his own Tamil Oh My Kadavule featured a Venkatesh cameo as the titular god.
c: "Disappointed husband asks god to restart his marriage. God appears in a suit and a beard. Marriage gets reset three times."

f: Boyapati Srinu's mass-masala film was NTR Jr.'s Ugadi-2012 release and paired him with Trisha and Karthika Nair.
c: "Software engineer in London inherits his grandfather's village feud. Flies back for a wedding. Kills a dozen uncles."

f: V. V. Vinayak's dual-role film grossed over 45 crore and featured NTR Jr. as both a Hyderabad thug and a Kuchipudi dancer.
c: "Twins split at birth: one runs Hyderabad's underworld, one dances classical. They swap lives over a single weekend."

f: Vinod Anantoju's debut, starring Anand Devarakonda, won the Gaddar Award for Third Best Feature and released directly on Amazon Prime.
c: "Guntur boy tries to open a tiffin centre in the city. Fails slowly. Returns home slower. The marriage is arranged meanwhile."

f: Mahi V. Raghav's Yatra sequel dramatised Jagan Mohan Reddy's 2017-19 padayatra and released weeks before the 2024 AP elections.
c: "Son walks 3,648 kilometres to claim his dead father's political party. Journalists follow him in air-conditioned vans."

f: Prasanth Varma's debut anthology used overlapping timelines and tackled child abuse, drug addiction, and queer identity in one film.
c: "Cafe in Hyderabad. Six unconnected stories play out across one evening. By the end every story is the same story."

f: Saagar K Chandra's 90s-set cop thriller went on to become the director's calling card for Virata Parvam and Bangarraju.
c: "1990s Hyderabad encounter specialist chases a cricketer-turned-rowdy. Alleyways keep switching. Both men carry the same brand of pistol."

f: K. Viswanath shot dance sequences at Valley of Flowers National Park and the Shanti Stupa. Choreographed by Kelucharan Mohapatra.
c: "Kuchipudi guru's daughter refuses to dance. Hoarding-painter next door sketches her anyway. She keeps finding her portrait on walls."

f: K. Viswanath's film gave lyricist Chembolu Seetharama Sastry his screen name Sirivennela after its title song.
c: "Blind flautist and a mute painter live in the same colony. They communicate through a schoolgirl and moonlight alone."

f: K. Viswanath's anti-dowry film was an early Chiranjeevi lead role and emphasised the dignity of labour over caste pride.
c: "Graduate takes a waiter's job at a mess. Fights a rich father for refusing dowry. Serves sambar while negotiating marriage."

f: K. Viswanath's film gave Chiranjeevi one of his rare art-film leads and remains a staple of Andhra school curriculums.
c: "Orphan cobbler adopts a street child. Works every hour for her school fees. Refuses every handout on principle."

f: K. Balachander's film won the National Film Award for Best Feature on National Integration and gave Chiranjeevi a rare art lead.
c: "Musician's son abandons classical Carnatic tradition to teach music to a Dalit village. Father refuses to acknowledge him for years."

f: K. Viswanath's film swept eight Nandi Awards and won Filmfare Best Director. Dubbed in Tamil as Isaikku Oru Koil.
c: "Classical vocalist and his mridangist wife drift apart over differing ragas. A prodigy daughter arrives. So does the tabla."

f: K. Viswanath's film examined conservatism and inter-caste marriage through the framework of the seven Hindu wedding vows.
c: "Brahmin priest's grandson falls for an untouchable girl. The village counts seven vows. The family counts seven insults."

f: Kodandarami Reddy's Chiranjeevi actioner ran 100 days in 25 centres and was a crucial post-Gharana-Mogudu consolidation hit.
c: "Harbour-loading chief-coolie fights the corrupt port contractor who killed his father. Lifts sacks. Lifts grudges. Lifts villains."

f: Kranthi Madhav's Valentine's-week release took its title from a song in the 1986 film Rakshasudu.
c: "Student union president loses a girl to an arranged marriage. Years later reads her diary. Says the line aloud again."

f: Mohana Krishna Indraganti's meta-romance paired Sudheer Babu with Aditi Rao Hydari and was set in the world of film production.
c: "Cartoonist hates film stars. A visiting star rents his parents' guesthouse. He draws her every day without admitting it."

f: Karuna Kumar's debut made Film Companion's top 7 Telugu films of 2020 and dramatised caste violence in Srikakulam's cashew belt.
c: "Two folk-musician Dalit brothers in a Srikakulam cashew town get swallowed by local caste politics. Years later they still carry knives to gigs."

f: Dasari Narayana Rao's Vijayashanti vehicle premiered at the 1998 International Film Festival of India in the mainstream section.
c: "Telangana woman from a bonded-labour family burns down the landlord's granary. Runs for the forest. Comes back with a scythe."

f: Krishna Vamsi's film explored Naxalism in Andhra and gave supporting roles to an early-career Ravi Teja and Soundarya.
c: "University student goes home for a funeral. Joins the forest brigade instead of the bus back. Three friends disappear one by one."

f: Rahul Ravindran's directorial debut won the National Film Award for Best Original Screenplay.
c: "Two strangers are introduced at a pre-wedding lunch. They spend the afternoon debating whether to say no. Parents pace outside."

f: Hasith Goli's directorial debut revolved around a serial liar running a xerox shop and crossed 20 crore despite a pandemic release.
c: "Xerox-shop assistant lies on every job application. Lies to his wife. Lies to his in-laws. Photocopies his own alibis."

f: Hanu Raghavapudi's debut was co-produced by S. S. Rajamouli and launched Naveen Chandra, Lavanya Tripathi, and Rahul Ravindran in Telugu.
c: "Biker girl is loved by two men in the same hill town. She loves one. She marries neither. Everyone ends up writing letters."

f: Sukumar wrote this with Palnati Surya Pratap directing. The film was banned in some territories for its candid depiction of casual sex.
c: "Catering-service chef falls for an upstairs model. Accepts her past. Then a colony uncle starts gossiping. He stops accepting."

f: T. Krishna's film made Vijayashanti an anti-establishment icon and was remade in Hindi as Pratighaat with Sujata Mehta.
c: "College lecturer's husband is killed by a local politician. She files a case. The case is dismissed. She picks up a sickle."

f: Siva Ananth's directorial debut paired ANR with grandson Siddharth and shot song sequences in Innsbruck.
c: "College senior is loved by three women at once. Announces this to his grandfather. Grandfather suggests he pick the quiet one."

f: Merlapaka Gandhi's direct Telugu remake of the Hindi Andhadhun released on Disney+ Hotstar and starred Nithiin as a blind pianist.
c: "Pretend-blind pianist stumbles into a star-actor's murder. Keeps the act up through the police questioning. The act stops being an act."

f: Muthyala Subbaiah's family drama paired Balakrishna with Laila and was a commercial success despite a mid-summer release.
c: "Village teacher raises his dead sister's child as his own. Rich grandmother arrives with lawyers. Court gets interrupted by a wedding card."
